Compare two coefficients via a z-score test

Source: R/compare_coefficients.R
compare_coefs.Rd

Tests whether two effect estimates differ, given their standard errors, under the assumption of independent estimates. Implements the z-score approach described in doi:10.1038/s41380-019-0596-9 .

Usage

compare_coefs(b1, b2, se1, se2)

Arguments

b1, b2

Numeric scalars: the two coefficient estimates.

se1, se2

Numeric scalars: standard errors of the two estimates. Must be positive.

Value

A named list with:

  • z_statistic — the z-score for the difference.

  • p_value — two-tailed p-value.

  • diff — b1 - b2.

  • se_diff — \(\sqrt{se1^2 + se2^2}\).

Examples

compare_coefs(b1 = 0.50, b2 = 0.30, se1 = 0.10, se2 = 0.12)
#> $z_statistic
#> [1] 1.280369
#> 
#> $p_value
#> [1] 0.2004155
#> 
#> $diff
#> [1] 0.2
#> 
#> $se_diff
#> [1] 0.156205
#>
